title: Azure Key-Vault access-policy / RBAC posture
description: Per-Key-Vault secrets-management + least-privilege posture captured read-only from Azure Resource Manager (ARM Reader role): the access model (legacy access policies vs Azure RBAC authorization), purge-protection + soft-delete state, public-network-access posture, the network-ACL default action, and the principals entitled to the vault (legacy access-policy entries with their granted keys/secrets/certificates permission VERBS, or RBAC role-assignment principals with their role name). The minimum that demonstrates secrets-management / least-privilege posture (e.g. the vault enforces purge protection and grants no over-broad secret access). Management-plane CONFIGURATION + access-policy / role-assignment METADATA only — NEVER a secret, key, or certificate VALUE: the connector reads only the ARM management plane (vault list + access policies) and NEVER the Key-Vault data plane (vault.azure.net secret/key/certificate get); it is never granted any data-plane permission. The platform evaluator decides pass/fail per (control, vault); the connector emits a descriptive default verdict. x-default-scf-anchors are default mapping hints flagged for maintainer recheck (OQ #9).

vault_id.description: ARM resource id of the Key Vault.

rbac_authorization.description: Vault uses Azure RBAC authorization (true) rather than legacy access policies (false).

purge_protection.description: Purge protection is enabled (a soft-deleted vault/secret cannot be permanently purged before the retention period elapses).

soft_delete_enabled.description: Soft-delete is enabled (deleted vaults/secrets are recoverable within the retention period).

public_network_access.description: Public-network-access posture (Enabled | Disabled); empty when the ARM resource did not report it.

network_acl_default.description: Network-ACL default action (Allow | Deny); empty when no network ACLs are configured.

access_entries.description: Principals entitled to the vault. For a legacy-access-policy vault each entry is an access policy (principal_id + the granted permission VERBS); for an RBAC vault each entry is a role assignment (principal_id + role_name). Access METADATA only — never a secret/key/certificate value.

principal_id.description: Object id of the entitled principal.

principal_type.description: Whether this entitlement is a legacy access-policy entry or an Azure RBAC role assignment.

permissions.description: Granted access-policy permission verbs, namespaced as '<area>:<verb>' (e.g. secrets:get, keys:list). Empty for an RBAC role assignment. Permission NAMES only — never a secret value.

role_name.description: RBAC role definition name (e.g. Key Vault Reader). Empty for a legacy access-policy entry.
